summary: Matched several small previously stubbed functions across Kirby fighter code, Rainbow Cruise stage code, and Samus grapple item code. The PR replaced decomp placeholder comments with simple wrappers or animation callbacks using existing engine helpers, with minimal include additions needed for inline access and external declarations.
what_changed: Implemented ftKb_SpecialAirS_Anim in ftKb_Init.c. The function pads the stack with PAD_STACK(0x08), checks ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj), and calls ftCo_Fall_Enter(gobj) when no animation frames remain.
Implemented ftKb_SkSpecialNCancel_Anim in ftKb_Init.c. It fetches the Fighter with GET_FIGHTER(gobj), clears fp->fv.kb.xB8, and calls ft_8008A2BC(gobj) when animation frames are exhausted.
Implemented grRCruise_801FF3B4 in grrcruise.c. It uses GET_GROUND(gobj), then calls grAnime_801C8138 with the object cast to HSD_GObj*, the ground map_id, and 0.
Added gr/inlines.h to grrcruise.c so GET_GROUND can be used.
Implemented it_802BAA58 in itsamusgrapple.c. It pads the stack with PAD_STACK(16), calls Item_80268E5C((HSD_GObj*) gobj, 4, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE), then calls it_802A2428(gobj).
Added itlinkhookshot.h and it/item.h includes in itsamusgrapple.c, apparently to expose item helper declarations/constants used by the new function.
smart_moves: Targeted small, isolated placeholder functions whose behavior could be expressed entirely with already-known helpers and inlines.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack layout explicitly with PAD_STACK in ftKb_SpecialAirS_Anim and it_802BAA58 rather than hiding stack differences behind logic changes.
Used established object access macros such as GET_FIGHTER and GET_GROUND instead of manually casting or reaching into gobj internals.
Kept the implementations minimal and callback-like, matching the common Melee pattern of animation callbacks ending state transitions when ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining returns 0.
Added only local includes needed by the newly matched functions, avoiding broad header churn.
decomp_lessons: Small animation callbacks often match as a simple frame-remaining check followed by a state transition helper.
For fighter callbacks, GET_FIGHTER should be preferred when accessing fighter variables such as fp->fv.kb.xB8.
For ground callbacks, include gr/inlines.h and use GET_GROUND when reading Ground fields like map_id.
For item callbacks involving animation changes, Item_80268E5C with ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE may need item/item.h, and item-specific helper calls may need sibling item headers.
PAD_STACK values can be necessary even for very small functions; do not remove them just because the C logic looks complete.
Stub replacement PRs can span unrelated subsystems when each function is tiny and independently matchable, but evidence in this slice does not show a shared semantic theme beyond being easy matches.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter functions use the ftKb_ prefix, with action/state names embedded in the symbol such as ftKb_SpecialAirS_Anim and ftKb_SkSpecialNCancel_Anim.
Rainbow Cruise stage functions use the grRCruise_ prefix followed by address-derived suffixes when semantic names are not yet known.
Unknown item functions retain address-style names such as it_802BAA58 when no better semantic name is evidenced.
GObj typedefs are subsystem-specific at the API boundary: Fighter_GObj*, Ground_GObj*, and Item_GObj*.
Engine object casts to HSD_GObj* are explicit at helper call sites when required by the callee signature.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0x08) in ftKb_SpecialAirS_Anim to satisfy stack-layout matching.
Used PAD_STACK(16) in it_802BAA58 to satisfy stack-layout matching.
Matched zero-frame animation endings with the idiom: if (ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) == 0) { transition_helper(gobj); }.
Matched ground animation setup by passing (HSD_GObj*) gobj, gp->map_id, and a literal 0 to grAnime_801C8138.
Matched item animation update by passing literal animation/state value 4 and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE to Item_80268E5C before invoking it_802A2428.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
No human PR body text was present in the provided excerpt, so intent beyond matching a few easy functions is inferred only from the title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find other ftKb animation callbacks that use ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining followed by ftCo_Fall_Enter or ft_8008A2BC.
Search for other Rainbow Cruise functions calling grAnime_801C8138 with Ground map_id.
Search for item functions using Item_80268E5C with ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE and follow-up item helper calls.
Look for remaining placeholder comments near ftKb_SkSpecialNLoop_Anim, ftKb_SkSpecialNEnd_Anim, grRCruise_801FF2C8, it_802BAA08, and it_802BAA94.
